name: plainmark
description: Free WYSIWYG Markdown editor with Obsidian-style live preview for VS Code Desktop and vscode.dev — rich-text editing for tables, math, diagrams, and callouts.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Workspace-defined plainmark.styles and plainmark.tableKeybindings are ignored until the workspace is trusted, because both inject content into the editor webview.

enumDescriptions: Adapts to the active VS Code color theme
GitHub light palette, regardless of the VS Code color theme
GitHub dark palette, regardless of the VS Code color theme
Anthropic-inspired warm cream and terracotta palette, regardless of the VS Code color theme
plainmark.theme.markdownDescription: Built-in theme for the Plainmark editor. `default` adapts to the VS Code color theme; the GitHub themes apply a fixed palette. CSS from `#plainmark.styles#` still overrides the theme. Pick via the **Plainmark: Select Theme** command.

plainmark.styles.markdownDescription: CSS files applied to the Plainmark preview. Accepts: workspace-relative paths (e.g. `./.vscode/plainmark.css`), absolute filesystem paths, and `file:` URIs. **v1 limitations**: `https://` and `http://` are declined (supply-chain surface); `@font-face` with remote `url()` is unsupported. User-scope entries are overridden by workspace-scope entries; both override Plainmark's built-in defaults. **Stability**: only `--plainmark-*` CSS variables are stable theming API — CSS class names and DOM structure are internal and may change in any release. See `docs/theming-guide.md`.

plainmark.tableKeybindings.markdownDescription: Keyboard shortcuts for table structural operations, active while editing a table cell. Keys use CodeMirror syntax — `Mod` (⌘ on macOS, Ctrl elsewhere), `Alt` (⌥ on macOS), `Shift`, `Ctrl` — joined with `-`, e.g. `Mod-Shift-Backspace`. Every binding must include a modifier; set a value to `""` to unbind. Recognized actions (defaults in parentheses): `insert_row_above` (`Alt-Shift-ArrowUp`), `insert_row_below` (`Mod-Enter`), `insert_column_left` (`Alt-Shift-ArrowLeft`), `insert_column_right` (`Alt-Shift-ArrowRight`), `delete_row` (`Mod-Shift-Backspace`), `delete_column` (unbound), `delete_table` (unbound), `swap_row_up` (`Alt-ArrowUp`), `swap_row_down` (`Alt-ArrowDown`), `swap_column_left` (`Alt-ArrowLeft`), `swap_column_right` (`Alt-ArrowRight`), `align_left`/`align_center`/`align_right`/`align_none` (unbound). Values that are invalid, modifier-less, duplicated, or that collide with editing/navigation keys (`Tab`, `Enter`, arrows, `Mod-z`) are ignored with a warning. Changing this setting reloads the editor view.

plainmark.imagePasteLocation.markdownDescription:
Folder where images pasted into the Plainmark editor are saved. Relative paths resolve against the current document's folder; the default `.` saves the image next to the document. Two variables are supported, matching VS Code's Markdown image-copy setting (`markdown.copyFiles.destination`):

- `${documentWorkspaceFolder}` — the workspace folder that contains the document; falls back to the document's folder when it is not in a workspace.
- `${documentBaseName}` — the document's file name without its extension.

For example, `${documentWorkspaceFolder}/assets` saves every image under a single workspace `assets` folder, while `assets/${documentBaseName}` gives each note its own image folder. Pasted files are named `image-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.<ext>` (the extension comes from the image type) and never overwrite an existing file. Pasting an image is unavailable in unsaved (untitled) documents and on read-only file systems.

scripts.test:integration:desktop: pnpm run build:dev && pnpm run test:integration:desktop:build && node tests/integration/electron/run-tests.mjs
scripts.test:integration:web: pnpm run build:dev && pnpm run test:integration:web:build && node tests/integration/web/run-tests.mjs

simple-git-hooks.pre-commit: pnpm run lint && pnpm run test:unit && pnpm run test:source-preservation && pnpm run build:check
